summary: Comprehensive bug review and Linux distribution compatibility analysis of dockym v0.1.0

critical_bugs.title: Compose project name mismatch causes services to appear as 'not running'
critical_bugs.description: The scanner uses dir_path.name as the project name (line 169), but Docker Compose uses the 'name' field from the compose file or derives it from the directory. When a compose file has a custom 'name' field (e.g., name: my-app), Docker labels containers with com.docker.compose.project=my-app, but the scanner creates a project named after the directory. The enrichment step in _enrich_with_docker_state won't match them, causing all services to appear as 'not running' even when they are.
critical_bugs.impact: Services show wrong status on every project with a custom compose 'name' field
critical_bugs.fix: Parse the compose file's top-level 'name' field and use it as the project key. Also try to read it from running containers via com.docker.compose.project label as fallback.

critical_bugs.title: Docker socket fallback only checks /var/run/docker.sock
critical_bugs.description: The Docker client fallback after from_env() failure only tries unix:///var/run/docker.sock. This misses many common Docker socket locations: Snap Docker (/var/snap/docker/run/docker.sock), Rootless Docker (/run/user/<uid>/docker.sock), Podman Docker compat (/run/user/<uid>/podman/podman.sock).
critical_bugs.impact: Docker connection fails for Snap, Rootless, and Podman users even though Docker is running
critical_bugs.fix: Try multiple socket paths in order: DOCKER_HOST env, /var/run/docker.sock, /var/snap/docker/run/docker.sock, /run/user/<uid>/docker.sock, /run/user/<uid>/podman/podman.sock

critical_bugs.title: Docker Compose V1 (docker-compose) not supported
critical_bugs.description: The code hardcodes 'docker compose' (V2 subcommand) with no fallback to 'docker-compose' (V1). Many older Linux distributions (Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, Debian 11) only ship Docker Compose V1. The app will crash with FileNotFoundError when trying to run compose operations.
critical_bugs.impact: All compose operations fail on systems with only Docker Compose V1 installed
critical_bugs.fix: Check for 'docker compose' availability first, fall back to 'docker-compose'. Use shutil.which() to detect.

critical_bugs.title: Thread-safety issues in DockerEventsWorker
critical_bugs.description: The _running bool and _client reference are accessed from both the worker thread (run()) and the main thread (stop()) without any synchronization. While CPython's GIL makes single bool reads/writes atomic, this is not guaranteed by the Python specification. More importantly, stop() can close the client while run() is iterating events, and _client is set to None in the finally block while stop() might be reading it.
critical_bugs.impact: Potential crash or undefined behavior on non-CPython implementations (PyPy, GraalPy)
critical_bugs.fix: Use threading.Event for _running flag, and protect _client access with a lock

bugs.title: Signal name typos: event_ocurred/error_ocurred
bugs.description: Signal names use 'ocurred' instead of 'occurred'. While consistent internally, this is a naming bug that will confuse contributors and may cause confusion in signal debugging.
bugs.impact: Developer confusion, broken external tools that inspect signal names
bugs.fix: Rename to event_occurred and error_occurred, update all connect() calls in main_window.py

bugs.title: EnvDialog._save_env assumes parent is QMainWindow
bugs.description: self.parent().statusBar().showMessage(...) assumes the parent widget is a QMainWindow with a status bar. If the parent is any other widget type, this raises AttributeError.
bugs.impact: Crash when saving .env if dialog parent changes
bugs.fix: Use isinstance() check or use QApplication.instance().topLevelWidgets() to find the main window, or emit a signal instead

bugs.title: _format_ports logic is wrong for long-form ports without published
bugs.description: When a compose port entry has only 'target' (container port) without 'published' (host port), the code produces 'target:target' instead of just 'target'. The fallback logic is inverted: entry.get('published', entry.get('target', '')) returns target when published is missing, and entry.get('target', entry.get('published', '')) also returns target, producing 'target:target'.
bugs.impact: Ports display incorrectly for containers without fixed host port mappings
bugs.fix: Check if 'published' exists separately: if only 'target', show just target. If 'published' exists, show 'published:target'

bugs.title: LogsWorker/ExecWorker can race and produce stale output
bugs.description: Each call to _load_logs creates a new LogsWorker without canceling the previous one. If the user clicks 'Refresh' rapidly, multiple workers run simultaneously. The old worker's signal (log_line) is still connected and could overwrite the newer worker's output, or both outputs get interleaved.
bugs.impact: Log display shows mixed/stale output when refresh is clicked rapidly
bugs.fix: Disconnect and delete old worker before creating a new one, or use a single worker that cancels previous operations

bugs.title: Prune dialog button uses wrong object name for styling
bugs.description: btn_prune.setObjectName('btn-stop') but there is no QSS rule for #btn-stop. The button should use 'btn-danger' for the red danger styling, or 'btn-primary' for a prominent action button.
bugs.fix: Change to setObjectName('btn-danger') for consistent danger styling

bugs.title: exec_run hardcodes 'sh -c' which may not exist in containers
bugs.description: exec_run uses 'sh -c command' which assumes sh is available in the container. Alpine containers use ash, some minimal containers (distroless, scratch) have no shell at all. This will fail with 'executable file not found' for those containers.
bugs.impact: Exec command fails silently or with confusing error on minimal containers
bugs.fix: Try 'sh -c' first, fall back to just the command directly, or allow user to specify shell

bugs.title: Config save is not atomic - corruption on crash
bugs.description: CONFIG_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(...)) is not atomic. If the app crashes or is killed during the write, the config file will be corrupted (truncated JSON).
bugs.impact: Config file corruption requires manual fix
bugs.fix: Write to a temp file first, then os.rename() atomically. Or use a write-then-rename pattern.

bugs.title: Extra keys in config file cause total config loss
bugs.description: cls(**data) will raise TypeError if the config file contains extra keys (e.g., from a newer version of the app). The TypeError is caught and defaults are returned, meaning ALL saved config is silently lost.
bugs.impact: User loses all config when upgrading to a new version that adds fields
bugs.fix: Use cls(**{k: v for k, v in data.items() if k in cls.__dataclass_fields__}) to filter unknown keys

bugs.title: _yaml_cache is not thread-safe
bugs.description: The _yaml_cache dictionary is accessed from multiple QThreadPool worker threads without any synchronization. While CPython's GIL provides some protection, concurrent dict operations (like eviction while another thread is iterating) can cause issues.
bugs.impact: Potential KeyError or RuntimeError on concurrent cache access
bugs.fix: Use threading.Lock to protect cache access, or use a thread-safe dict from concurrent.futures

bugs.title: Fast refresh accesses private scanner internals
bugs.description: _fast_refresh imports and calls _enrich_with_docker_state from the scanner module. This couples the UI to the scanner's private implementation. If the scanner's internal API changes, this will break silently.
bugs.impact: Fragile coupling between UI and engine layers
bugs.fix: Create a public refresh_enriched() function in the scanner module, or use the existing scan_paths() with a flag

bugs.title: Default font 'Inter' is not installed on most Linux distros
bugs.description: The default font_family is 'Inter' which is not installed by default on most Linux distributions. The fallback chain includes 'Segoe UI' (Windows-only) and 'SF Pro' (macOS-only) which are wasted entries on Linux.
bugs.impact: UI renders with generic sans-serif font, looking different than intended
bugs.fix: Add common Linux fonts to the fallback: 'Cantarell', 'Noto Sans', 'DejaVu Sans'. Consider detecting installed fonts at startup.

bugs.title: QSS text-transform: uppercase is not supported by Qt
bugs.description: Qt's CSS engine does not support the 'text-transform' property. The uppercase section labels (e.g., 'SERVICIO', 'ACCIONES') will NOT be rendered in uppercase. They will appear as-is.
bugs.impact: Section labels show raw text instead of uppercase
bugs.fix: Use .upper() in Python when setting the text, or use unicode uppercase characters

bugs.title: QSS letter-spacing is not supported by Qt
bugs.description: Qt's CSS engine does not support 'letter-spacing'. All letter-spacing values in the QSS are silently ignored.
bugs.impact: Typography doesn't match the design spec
bugs.fix: Remove letter-spacing properties or use QApplication font tracking to adjust

bugs.title: Hardcoded 'monospace' font name in QFont
bugs.description: QFont('monospace', 10) uses 'monospace' which is not a standard Qt font family name. Qt will attempt to match it but results vary across systems. Should use a specific font or a recognized generic.
bugs.impact: Monospace font may not be found on some systems
bugs.fix: Use QFontDatabase.systemFont(QFontDatabase.FixedFont) or try specific fonts: 'Fira Code', 'JetBrains Mono', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'monospace'

bugs.title: No HiDPI rounding policy set
bugs.description: On fractional DPI scaling (125%, 150%), Qt6 without HighDpiScaleFactorRoundingPolicy can produce blurry or misaligned rendering.
bugs.impact: Blurry UI on HiDPI displays with fractional scaling

bugs.title: XDG_CONFIG_HOME not respected
bugs.description: Config directory is hardcoded to ~/.config/dockym instead of using $XDG_CONFIG_HOME or platformdirs. On systems where XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set to a non-standard location, the config won't be found.
bugs.impact: Config not found on systems with custom XDG_CONFIG_HOME

bugs.title: closeEvent double-processing on fade animation
bugs.description: closeEvent calls _quit_app() which stops the refresh timer. If the user clicks close during a fade animation that's already in progress, _closing_animation is not None so _quit_app() returns early. But the second closeEvent also calls _quit_app() and then event.ignore(). This means the window stays open during the animation.
bugs.impact: Minor UX issue: window stays visible during close animation
bugs.fix: Check _closing_animation state in closeEvent and just ignore if already closing

docker_compose_version.issue: Only Docker Compose V2 (docker compose) is supported

font_availability.issue: Default font 'Inter' not installed by default on most Linux distros

system_tray.issue: System tray support varies by desktop environment and display server

affected_combinations.note: GNOME removed built-in system tray support. Requires AppIndicator extension or SNI host.
Full system tray support on both X11 and Wayland.
System tray works on X11. Wayland support varies.

ubuntu: Ensure docker-compose-plugin package is installed for V2 support
Install fonts-inter package for the default font
For Snap Docker users, add /var/snap/docker/run/docker.sock as socket fallback
fedora: docker-compose-plugin is available in default repos
Consider Podman compatibility for rootless containers
arch_linux: Inter font available via inter-font AUR package
Wayland users may need xdg-desktop-portal for system tray
debian: Backport or install docker-compose-plugin for V2 support
Inter font available via fonts-inter package
Older releases (Bullseye) may only have Docker Compose V1
alpine: Minimal Docker packages, may not have compose
Limited font availability, will fall back to generic sans-serif
Consider as a Docker container target, not a host OS

priority_ranking.title: Compose project name mismatch
priority_ranking.reason: Affects every project with a custom compose 'name' field. Services appear stopped when running.

priority_ranking.title: Docker socket fallback too narrow
priority_ranking.reason: Breaks Docker connection for Snap, Rootless, and Podman users.

priority_ranking.title: Docker Compose V1 not supported
priority_ranking.reason: Crashes on older distros that only have docker-compose (V1).

priority_ranking.title: EnvDialog parent assumption
priority_ranking.reason: Crash on .env save if dialog hierarchy changes.

priority_ranking.title: Port display logic inverted
priority_ranking.reason: Incorrect port display for services without fixed host mappings.

priority_ranking.title: QSS text-transform not supported
priority_ranking.reason: Section labels don't render as uppercase.

priority_ranking.title: Default font Inter not available
priority_ranking.reason: UI uses generic sans-serif on most distros.

quick_wins: Replace text-transform: uppercase with .upper() in Python (BUG-016)
Add Linux fonts to fallback chain: Cantarell, Noto Sans, DejaVu Sans (BUG-015)
Filter unknown keys in Config.load() instead of losing all config (BUG-012)
important_fixes: Add multiple Docker socket path fallbacks (BUG-002)
Add Docker Compose V1 fallback detection (BUG-003)
Parse compose file 'name' field for correct project matching (BUG-001)
Cancel previous worker before creating new one in LogsDialog (BUG-008)
nice_to_have: Create public scanner API instead of using _enrich_with_docker_state (BUG-014)
Try different shells in exec_run instead of hardcoding sh (BUG-010)
